title: Symmetric Tree
description:
Given a binary tree, check whether it is a mirror of itself (i.e., symmetric around its center). A tree is symmetric if the left subtree is a mirror reflection of the right subtree. You can implement this by checking if the left and right subtrees are mirrors of each other recursively. TreeNode is defined as follows:
class TreeNode { int val; TreeNode left; TreeNode right; }

examples.explanation: The tree is symmetric because the left subtree [2,3,4] is a mirror reflection of the right subtree [2,4,3].
The left subtree [2,null,3] is not a mirror reflection of the right subtree [2,3,null], hence the tree is not symmetric.
A single node tree is symmetric as there are no children to compare.

constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100].
The value of a node is an integer.
hints: Consider using a recursive approach to compare the left and right subtrees.
You may want to write a helper function that checks if two trees are mirrors of each other.
Pay attention to null nodes; they can be part of a symmetric structure.
Think about edge cases such as empty trees or trees with only one node.
